name: No Large Functions (Python)
description: Python functions exceeding the configured line limit are harder to read and maintain.
rationale: Large functions do too much. The Zen of Python states 'Flat is better than nested' and 'Simple is better than complex'. Breaking long functions into smaller ones with descriptive names acts as self-documentation and simplifies testing.
noncompliant_code:
def process_report(data):
    # 50+ lines of parsing, transforming,
    # validating, formatting, and writing
    # all in one function
compliant_code:
def process_report(data):
    parsed = parse_input(data)
    validated = validate_entries(parsed)
    formatted = format_report(validated)
    write_output(formatted)
